lint.command: ROOT="/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control" && cd "$ROOT" && bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh && bash ci/check-architecture.sh && bash ci/check-enterprise-policy.sh && bash ci/check-orchestrator-layer.sh && python3 scripts/check_flaky_tags.py --tests-root erp-domain/src/test/java --gate gate-fast && bash scripts/guard_openapi_contract_drift.sh

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a dispatch DTO parity guard or equivalent review rule for shared handoff/library docs that enumerate `DispatchConfirmationResponse` fields, because route-literal-only guards still would not detect response-shape drift in `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The re-review for `dispatch-frontend-handoff-response-alignment` passed only after a manual comparison between `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md`, `DispatchConfirmationResponse.java`, and `openapi.json`, while `scripts/guard_workflow_canonical_paths.sh` still checks only route/ownership literals.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify `.factory/library/*` docs-only validation expectations and how workers should report skill adherence, so feature-specific docs-lint plus contract-guard packets are not unnecessarily escalated to `gate_fast.sh` while still being marked as procedure-following.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The re-review for `dispatch-frontend-handoff-response-alignment` found the worker ran `bash scripts/gate_fast.sh` even though `features.json` required only `docs-lint`, `contract-guards`, and manual DTO/OpenAPI comparison, and the handoff still reported `followedProcedure=true`.
